questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Smart Warehouse Management System?

answers.b: To automate and optimize warehouse operations

explanations.a: Smart systems aim to digitize and reduce paperwork.
explanations.b: Automation and optimization are the core pillars of smart warehousing.
explanations.c: Smart systems optimize rather than reduce capacity.
explanations.d: Inventory tracking is enhanced, not replaced.

questions.question: Which Industry 4.0 technology is commonly used for automated material transportation in warehouses?

explanations.a: PLCs control machines but don't transport materials directly.
explanations.b: Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) transport materials without manual intervention.
explanations.c: CNC machines are used for subtractive manufacturing.
explanations.d: 3D printers are used for additive manufacturing.

explanations.a: The standard Industry 4.0 terminology is Automated Guided Vehicle.
explanations.b: AGV stands for Automated Guided Vehicle.
explanations.c: It is referred to as 'Guided', not 'Advanced'.
explanations.d: It is referred to as 'Vehicle', not 'Ground'.

answers.b: Percentage of warehouse space being used

explanations.b: Storage utilization represents the percentage of total storage capacity filled.
explanations.c: Robot fleet size is a hardware metric, not space utilization.

questions.question: Which parameter helps determine when inventory replenishment is required?

explanations.b: Warehouse efficiency is a system-wide KPI.
explanations.c: The inventory threshold triggers reorders when stock falls below a set limit.
explanations.d: Storage capacity is the total space limit.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a Warehouse Management System (WMS)?

explanations.a: Manufacturing is done by production systems.
explanations.b: A WMS is software that controls and optimizes warehouse operations like inventory tracking and storage allocation.
explanations.c: Payroll is managed by HR systems.
explanations.d: Payments are processed by financial systems.

questions.question: Which technology provides a virtual representation of a physical warehouse system?

explanations.a: A Digital Twin is a virtual representation of a physical system used for real-time monitoring and simulation.
explanations.b: Blockchain is for secure data recording.
Storage utilization is the percentage of total storage that is currently occupied.
explanations.d: This simply adds the two values.

questions.question: What is the definition of Safety Stock?

answers.a: Stock that is damaged and unsafe to use
answers.b: Inventory that is kept in a secure vault
answers.c: Extra inventory maintained to prevent stockouts during unexpected demand
answers.d: Stock that has passed safety inspections

explanations.a: Safety stock relates to quantity, not physical safety of the items.
explanations.b: It is not about physical security.
explanations.c: Safety stock provides a buffer against supply chain uncertainties and demand spikes.
explanations.d: It refers to reserve quantities, not safety standards.

questions.question: How does the Nearest Neighbor routing algorithm affect AGV travel time in the simulation?

answers.c: It has no effect on travel time

explanations.b: The Nearest Neighbor algorithm applies a multiplier of 0.80, effectively reducing travel time by 20%.
explanations.d: Balanced Load reduces congestion delays, not Nearest Neighbor.
